title: data-tw error envelope
description:
Wrapped client output produced by pack.py's wrap() helper. Every leaf in a TW pack is an instance of this envelope. UNIQUE TO TW: all 5 country layers wrap, but data-tw's envelope is the only one that uses _tier='2-gap' and the explicit _source enum below. The data-* layers in other countries DO NOT nest the underlying client output under a top-level wrapper of this exact shape.

Shape comes directly from scripts/pack.py wrap(): a success envelope has {_tier, _source, _action, data}. An error envelope has {_tier, _source, _action, _error} plus optional debug fields (_stderr, _cmd, _stdout_head) that come straight from run_client().

_tier.description: Provenance tier. A = primary primary-source (MOPS, TWSE OpenAPI, CBC, DGBAS, NDC, statgov). 2 = cross-source convenience (yfinance, FinMind .TWO history). 2-gap = by-design Tier-A gap supplier (FinMind T86 daily flow + 融資融券時序 — no Tier A endpoint exists).

_action.description: Client action name (e.g. 'company-basic', 'balance-sheet', 'T86', 'preset-bundle'). Free-form string; values are documented per-pack in references/output-schema-overview.md.

data.description: Present on success. Shape is per-action and intentionally not constrained at this layer — see per-pack schemas (schema-snapshot.json etc.) for typed leaves where they matter to consumers.

_error.description: Present on failure. One of: 'timeout', 'exit_<rc>', 'json_decode: <msg>', 'exec_failed: <exception>'. See pack.py run_client().

_stderr.description: Truncated subprocess stderr (first 600 chars). Present when _error == 'exit_<rc>'.

_cmd.description: The full subprocess command that failed. Present on every error envelope.

_stdout_head.description: First 300 chars of stdout when JSON decode fails. Present when _error starts with 'json_decode'.
